description: Lead a large-scale campus tour program serving K–8 students; directly managed 75 volunteers.
Increased number of guests served by 40% through improved capacity planning.
Designed a long-range scheduling and registration system extending school registration lead time from 2 weeks to 2 months, reducing no-show rates.
Built capacity forecasts using four years of historical data to publish conservative availability under uncertainty, releasing ~50% of final capacity in advance.
Served as primary liaison for over 100 school administrators, principals, and teachers.
Authored two operational manuals on municipal government alliances and strategic partnerships to support long-term organizational growth.
Worked in a Spanish-speaking environment; achieved advanced Spanish proficiency.

role: Cub Tours Training and Recruitment Chair
description: Redesigned guide training into a hybrid curriculum combining longer workshops with targeted modules, improving instructional coherence and outcomes.
Developed approximately 6 hours of classroom instruction and 8 hours of tour practice.
Onboarded 29 new guides in the most demographically diverse cohort since pre-COVID.
Conducted research for clients in clean materials, EV charging, quantum computing, and domestic manufacturing.
Authored a white paper for congressional audiences on the strategic importance of quantum computing investment.
Developed a GHG-emissions framework categorizing impacts across manufacturing stages.
Supported climate-focused clients by identifying federal grant opportunities and tax credits.
